t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 我会在这里继续等待，直到你做好准备。
t_en: I'll continue my wait here until you are fully prepared.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是否已经做好准备凝视「终末」的投影了？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you ready to gaze at the projection of Finality?

t_ch: 我该怎么做？
t_en: What should I do?

t_ch: 闭上双眼，请让我为你溯洄，「终末」的投影……
t_en: Please close your eyes and let me help you with the backtracking to see Finality's projection...

t_ch: 你闭上双眼，耳边响起送葬的哀歌。即使是联觉新标，也无法告诉你那些歌词的真实含义。你感到自己逐渐下沉，远离真实世界，但过去擦肩而过的死亡，却逐渐迫近。
t_en: You close your eyes and hear an elegy. Even the Synesthesia Beacon can't fathom the meaning of the lyrics. You can feel yourself sinking, getting further away from the real world. However, you find the deaths you once narrowly escaped drawing closer.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 我会在这里继续等待，直到你做好准备。
t_en: I'll continue my wait here until you are fully prepared.

t_ch: …你所经历的绝境，虽然未能让「终末」降临，却令祂的话语不再是呢喃，愈发清晰。
t_en: ...Although the desperate crises you have experienced did not bring about the Finality, it has made THEIR mumbling much clearer.

t_ch: 末日一定会降临吗？
t_en: Is the apocalypse inevitable?

t_ch: 那一天{RUBY_B#早已}必将{RUBY_E#}到来。
t_en: That day {RUBY_B#has already}will definitely{RUBY_E#} come.

t_ch: 这场战斗当时也没这么难啊？
t_en: This battle didn't feel so intense at that time.

t_ch: 因为真实的体验遭到了潜意识的篡改。最为恐怖的体验并非存在于外部世界，而诞生于灵魂中。
t_en: Your subconscious has tampered with the real experience. The most terrifying experiences do not exist in the outside world but within one's soul.

t_ch: 在刚才的某个瞬间，我确实地捕捉到了祂的呢喃。祂说，你{RUBY_B#已}将{RUBY_E#}与祂同行。就在{RUBY_B#不远的过去}不久的将来{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: At some point in time earlier, I definitely caught THEIR murmuring. THEY said you {RUBY_B#had joined}would join{RUBY_E#} THEM in THEIR journey {RUBY_B#not too long ago}in the near future{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 为什么是我？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 因为你受到祂的垂怜。
t_en: Because you have received THEIR grace.

t_ch: 你所行之处，伴随着祂的凝视。你所践行的，也是祂{RUBY_B#尚未}早已{RUBY_E#}宣告的谶语。繁星是祂的眼，微风是祂的手。你罹受的每场灾祸，皆是「终末」的启示。
t_en: Wherever you walk, you are under THEIR gaze. What you practice is also THEIR prophecy that has {RUBY_B#yet to be}already been{RUBY_E#} announced. The stars are THEIR eyes, and the breeze is THEIR hands. Every disaster that befalls you is a revelation of Finality.

t_ch: 我也奉行承诺，给予你相应的报酬…如果可以的话，还请您继续协助我。那些末日图景，能帮助我继续追寻无形的{RUBY_B#溯洄者}储君{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: I'll reward you as promised... If possible, please continue assisting me. Those images of the apocalypse can help my search for the {RUBY_B#one against the current}shapeless prince{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是否已经做好准备凝视「终末」的投影了？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you ready to gaze at the projection of Finality?

t_ch: 提供「末日幻影」
t_en: Provide Apocalyptic Shadows

t_ch: 进入「奇珍琳琅」
t_en: Enter Priceless Jewels

t_ch: 你还搜集了哪些末日的忆泡？
t_en: What other Memory Bubbles of the apocalypse are in your collection?

t_ch: 有些问题想问你…
t_en: I've got a few questions I'd like to ask you...

t_ch: 请问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 你究竟是什么人？
t_en: Who exactly are you?

t_ch: 我是「葬仪知宾」——是凋亡世界的记录者，亦是求索终末的循迹者。
t_en: I'm a Creed Exequy — the chronicler of the dying world, as well as the seeker of the traces of Finality.

t_ch: 你有什么目的？
t_en: What do you want?

t_ch: 迎接命定的邂逅，然后向无形的{RUBY_B#溯洄者}储君{RUBY_E#}发问——
t_en: To welcome my fated encounter. Then, I'll ask the {RUBY_B#one against the current}shapeless prince{RUBY_E#} some questions.

t_ch: 「如果祂想终结一切，为何向过去预警？如果祂想拯救一切，为何带来末日？如果一切终归寂灭，那我们存在的意义是什么？」
t_en: "Why send warnings to the past if THEY want to end everything? Why bring about the apocalypse if THEY want to salvage everything? What is the meaning of our existence if everything is bound to be reduced to nothingness in the end?"

t_ch: 「末王」究竟是什么？
t_en: What is Terminus?

t_ch: 祂是执掌「终末」的神明，从未来逆时而行。祂会出现于每个「终末」的瞬间，带来{RUBY_B#往昔的记录}未来的预言{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: THEY are the god of Finality, traveling against time from the future. THEY appear at every moment of Finality, bringing {RUBY_B#records of the past}prophecies of the future{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 「终末」是一切的终点吗？
t_en: Is Finality the end of everything?

t_ch: 不，「终末」令琥珀崩裂、永生枯败、旋律喑哑、光矢黯淡、黑日只余灰烬、酒馆落满灰尘——即使毁灭本身，亦不能免于毁灭。
t_en: No. Finality causes amber to rupture, immortality to wither, music to mute, light arrows to dim, the Dark Sun to be reduced to ashes, and the Tavern to be covered in dust. Even Destruction itself cannot be spared from destruction.

t_ch: 然而「终末」并非一切的终点。万事万物将由此始发，前往下一场「终末」。
t_en: Yet, Finality isn't the end of everything. All things will originate from there and move on to the next Finality.

t_ch: 我没其他问题了。
t_en: No further questions.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 悼亡诗沉默不语，身形几乎融入蜿蜒的影子中。
t_en: Elegy falls silent, their figure almost merging into the winding shadows.

t_ch: 你还搜集了哪些末日的忆泡？
t_en: What other Memory Bubbles of the apocalypse are in your collection?

t_ch: 我亲眼见证的末日么…让我想想。
t_en: The apocalypse I've witnessed myself? Let me think.

t_ch: 尼斯的人们偶尔会想起那个海洋耸立而起，吞下他们文明的日子。
t_en: From time to time, the people of Neese will recall the day the towering waves rose from the ocean and engulfed their civilization.

t_ch: 他们就像放肆的孩子，以为能永远逃离母亲的管束。
t_en: Like mischievous children, they thought they could escape their mother's control forever.

t_ch: 但当他们渴求海中的钢铁，燃烧起苍蓝的火焰，为了采掘而融化极地冰山时。
t_en: But when they ignited azure flames and melted the snow on the arctic mountains to mine the iron in the ocean...

t_ch: 母亲的怀抱重新淹没了他们。
t_en: Their mother's embrace drowned them once again.

t_ch: 只是这次的怀抱却并不温柔。
t_en: But this time, her embrace wasn't a gentle one.

t_ch: 你还搜集了哪些末日的忆泡？
t_en: What other Memory Bubbles of the apocalypse are in your collection?

t_ch: 我亲眼见证的末日么…让我想想。
t_en: The apocalypse I've witnessed myself? Let me think.

t_ch: 践行巡猎的仙舟追逐着丰饶赐福的子民，殊不知这最后的追逐却落入了精心布置的圈套。
t_en: The Xianzhou that follows The Hunt pursued those who were blessed by Abundance, only to find themselves falling into a carefully laid trap.

t_ch: 活体的赤红星宿笼罩在孤舟的穹顶，随着活物一般的搏动裂开了筋肉枯蔓构成的外壳，舟体被彻底包裹。
t_en: The living crimson star hovered in the sky over the singular ship. It pulsated like a living creature, causing the shell formed by flesh and withered vines to crack before enveloping the entire ship.

t_ch: 赤红的星辰如同巨兽的反刍的胃囊，也如同血肉铸造的温床滋生污秽。
t_en: The crimson star was akin to a behemoth's stomach in charge of regurgitation, as well as a hotbed of corruption made of flesh.

t_ch: 此刻，每个被吞噬的仙舟人只有一个诉求——死亡。
t_en: At that moment, every Xianzhou native who was swallowed had only one wish — death.

t_ch: 长生的赐福却成了诅咒。仙舟之民在反复的吞吐中经历无数次融解重构。
t_en: The blessing of immortality turned into a curse. The Xianzhou people underwent countless cycles of dissolution and re-structure in the repeated regurgitation.

t_ch: 最终个体意义不复存在，不死的赐福让敌我彼此也融合为一。
t_en: Ultimately, the concept of "individuals" is lost. The blessing of immortality joined friends and foes into one.

t_ch: 你还搜集了哪些末日的忆泡？
t_en: What other Memory Bubbles of the apocalypse are in your collection?

t_ch: 我亲眼见证的末日么…让我想想。
t_en: The apocalypse I've witnessed myself? Let me think.

t_ch: 边陲星系札乌姆，曾隶属于星际和平公司的物流路线的天体。
t_en: Zaum was once a border star system on an IPC logistic route.

t_ch: 受命运眷顾，他们出了一位先知。先知将不属于这个时代的知识带给了札乌姆，自此，被科技武装的他们有资格与公司坐在谈判桌上。
t_en: Blessed by luck, a prophet emerged, bringing knowledge ahead of the star system's time to Zaum. Starting from then, they had been equipped with cutting-edge technology that put them on the negotiation table with the IPC.

t_ch: 只是先知不知道，他开启的是封印灾难的箱子。
t_en: Unbeknownst to the prophet, he had opened a Pandora's box.

t_ch: 公司不会放任物流链中的一个星系能有资格与他们讨价还价。于是刺杀、离间、煽动对立等手段让这个星球的民众彼此枪口相向。
t_en: The IPC wouldn't give any star system in their logistics chain any room for horse-trading. By way of assassination, sowing discord, and inciting division, the IPC turned the people of Zaum against each other.

t_ch: 然而札乌姆并不是只有战争一种顽疾。凭空出现的灰色浪潮如同瘟疫蔓延。两个对立政权则彼此怀疑对方使用了违禁的超自然武器。
t_en: Yet, war wasn't the only bane Zaum was facing. A gray tide that emerged out of nowhere started spreading like a plague. Two opposing political forces suspected each other of using forbidden supernatural weapons.

t_ch: 一段时间后，所有人发现，能夺走一切活性的灰色浪潮，是针对整个星球的无差别疾病。
t_en: After some time, it came to the people's realization that the gray tide that left everything lifeless was a disease that afflicted all indiscriminately.

t_ch: 其实，灰潮产生于人祸。先知篡改历史后，消失的世界线凝结成海啸般的混沌忆质。只是谁也不愿意直面症结所在。
t_en: The truth is, the gray tide was a calamity caused by human folly. After the prophet altered history, the disappeared timelines condensed into a tsunami-like muddled memoria. Yet, no one was bold enough to directly address the underlying issue.

t_ch: 即使先知被刺杀，也并未能终止纠纷。一个充盈混沌忆质的洞，凭空地出现在两个政权的交界地上。
t_en: Even the assassination of the prophet wasn't enough to quell the chaos. A cavity filled with chaotic memoria appeared out of nowhere at the border between the two opposing forces.

t_ch: 究竟是谁最先发射了导弹已经无从考据了。人们只记得，明明本意终止忆质灾祸的飞弹却成了这场旷日持久战争的导火索。
t_en: There was no way to verify who first fired the missile. People only remember that the missile, originally intended to subdue the disaster of the memoria, became what started the prolonged war thereafter.

t_ch: 战火持续了两个琥珀纪，已知的国家最后只成为了地理概念。
t_en: The war lasted for two Amber Eras. Eventually, the established nation was reduced to a mere geographical concept.

t_ch: 这个世界幸运被透支。虽然没毁于战火，却溺毙在灰色的海啸中。
t_en: The world had overdrawn its fortune. It wasn't destroyed by the war but was drowned in the gray tsunami waves.

t_ch: 在灭亡前的人们也无意追责究竟是哪一方的错。只知道那个美好的世界早已一去不返。
t_en: With obliteration looming large, people couldn't care less to point fingers. The only thing they were certain was that the beautiful world would be gone forever.

t_ch: 按照爱德华医生提供的地址，你似乎找到了那位「合作伙伴」。
t_en: According to the address that Dr. Edward provided, you seem to have found that supposed "business partner."

t_ch: 你闭上双眼，耳边响起送葬的哀歌。你感到自己逐渐下沉，远离真实世界，但过去擦肩而过的死亡，却逐渐迫近。
t_en: You close your eyes and hear an elegy. You can feel yourself sinking, getting further away from the real world. However, you find the deaths you once narrowly escaped drawing closer.

t_ch: 很高兴见到你，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。你的到来就像早春的暖阳，让谬误的积雪尽数消融。
t_en: Glad to meet you, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}. Your presence brings a warmth akin to the spring sun, melting away the snow of falsehoods.

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆，你怎么在这儿？
t_en: What brings you here, Screwllum?

t_ch: 请问…你是？
t_en: And... you are?

t_ch: 天才俱乐部#76螺丝咕姆，模拟宇宙的开发者之一，黑塔应当向你提起过。
t_en: I'm Screwllum, Member #76 of the Genius Society and one of the developers of the Simulated Universe. Herta should have mentioned my name to you.

t_ch: 逻辑：我想以个人名义邀请你参与一场思想实验。
t_en: Logic: I'd like to personally invite you to a thought experiment.

t_ch: 以模拟宇宙的底层逻辑为基础，螺丝星的技术为载体，我编写了「差分宇宙」，作为分支项目为模拟宇宙承担一些独立的研究。
t_en: Utilizing the Simulated Universe's underlying logic and the Planet Screwllum technology, I have created the Divergent Universe as a branch project for independent research regarding the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 当前，差分宇宙正在运行某位博识学会的学者提出的课题。结论：思想的碰撞总是令人喜悦，我乐于见到更多学术伙伴。
t_en: Currently, the Divergent Universe is exploring a topic proposed by a certain scholar from the Intelligentsia Guild. Conclusion: It is always a delight to see a clash of thoughts, and I am pleased to have more research partners.

t_ch: 这人我认识吗？
t_en: Do I know this scholar?

t_ch: 请容我暂时保留答案，为故事点缀上些许神秘的花朵不失为一种浪漫。
t_en: Please allow me to keep the answer hidden from you, as a touch of mystery adds charm to the tale.

t_ch: 学会还有这等高手？
t_en: Is there such a talented scholar in the Guild?

t_ch: 智慧的原野上不止有「天才」的大树，每一朵花蕾的存在都不可或缺。
t_en: Wisdom's realm extends beyond the towering individual trees known geniuses. Each unassuming flower bud is essential.

t_ch: 请跟我来吧。因为空间站是黑塔的私人财产，差分宇宙的测试场所另在他处。为此，我动用了一些「银河贵宾」的特权。
t_en: Now, please come with me. Since the space station is Herta's personal asset, I have arranged the test site for the Divergent Universe elsewhere, utilizing some of my "Cosmic VIP" privileges.

t_ch: 「盛会之星」匹诺康尼，希望你会喜欢。
t_en: Welcome to Penacony, the Planet of Festivities. I hope you find it enjoyable.

t_ch: 你登上螺丝咕姆的舰船，来到金碧辉煌的梦之国度。一艘巨大的飞艇飘浮在忆质之海上，宛如一只初醒的飞鸟，在梦中荡起层层涟漪。
t_en: You board the Screwllum's ship, entering a dream realm of glittering splendor. A colossal airship floats on an ocean of memoria, like a bird awakening, stirring ripples through the dream.

t_ch: 当然，它现在的名字，应该是—— {TEXTJOIN#87}。
t_en: But of course, its current name is — {TEXTJOIN#87}.

t_ch: 我们到了。梦想之地的风景总是令人眼花缭乱，博识学会的那位先生对此颇有怨词。
t_en: Here we are. The scenery in the Land of the Dreams is always captivating, although that gentleman from the Intelligentsia Guild did not find it to suit his tastes.

t_ch: 我曾向他发出邀请，希望他能作为博识学会的代表，为天才们的研究增添一些外来血液。结论：他拒绝了。
t_en: I once invited him to bring fresh perspectives to the research conducted by the geniuses as a representative of the Intelligentsia Guild. Conclusion: He declined.

t_ch: 在交流中，这位先生提出了一个优美的类比：星神和凡人就像函数方程式的两端，只需解明一端的未知数，就能求出另一端的答案。
t_en: During our discussion, that gentleman offered a beautiful analogy: Aeons and mortals are like the two ends of a functional equation. By solving the unknowns at one end, we can discover the answer at the other.

t_ch: 寻索星神的记录如大海捞针，凡人的故事则遍地开花。他认为比起诉诸众神，命途上芸芸众生的足迹才是通往真理的不二法门。
t_en: The records of the Aeons are scarce, while the stories of mortals are abundant. He believes that rather than seeking the Aeons, following the traces left by ordinary people on the Paths is a better way to seek the truth.

t_ch: 「数百个琥珀纪以来，烛墨学派收集了大量的史料，它们完全公开。但仰望星空的『天才』们大概无暇关注这些在沟壑里腐烂的历史残渣。」
t_en: "Over countless Amber Eras, the Candelagraphos has amassed a wealth of historical material, openly accessible. However, the geniuses busy gazing at the stars might not spare any time to delve into these remnants of history rotting in the gutters of time."

t_ch: 这位先生的建议虽有些刺耳，但不无道理。
t_en: While that gentleman's suggestion may sound harsh, it is not without merit.

t_ch: 当然，黑塔表达了截然相反的看法。但那道神与人的方程式，就像是获得了生命一般在我的灵感回路中无限地蔓延、如花瓣展开。
t_en: Naturally, Herta expressed an opposing view. However, that potential equation of gods and mortals, as if imbued with life, expanded infinitely like petals within my circuits of inspiration.

t_ch: 借由把烛墨学派的档案引入差分宇宙的沙盒，以「凡人」作为演算的跟踪目标，构建出命途的模型，我将这一课题命名为：「人间喜剧」。
t_en: By incorporating the archives from the Candelagraphos into the sandbox for the Divergent Universe and by using "mortals" as the focus of the extrapolation, I have constructed models for Paths and named this research project "The Human Comedy."

t_ch: 我很期待，你会为这场思想实验带来怎样的新火花。这边请，朋友。
t_en: I eagerly await what new insights you will bring to this thought experiment. Please follow me, my friend.

t_ch: 感受如何，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}？
t_en: How did it feel, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我似乎变成了另一个人…
t_en: Felt like I became someone else...

t_ch: 星神向世间投下祝福，凡人也在命途的道路中向上攀登。在方程式的推演中，这些蛛网般的轨迹会在特定的位置交汇，获得单一的输出结果：一位具象化的命途行者。
t_en: The Aeons bestow their blessings upon the world, while mortals ascend various Paths. Through the extrapolation of equations, these web-like trajectories will converge at specific points to generate a singular result — a materialized Pathstrider.

t_ch: 我将你的意识分离成两部分，其一作为在命途上行走的「自我」，在演算中走向相异的结点；其二作为锚定认知的「他者」，化身观测者飘浮在上层。
t_en: I have divided your consciousness into two parts: One as the Ego, traversing the Path and progressing towards distinct nodes in the algorithm. The second as the Other, becoming an observer that floats on a higher level.

t_ch: 这样处理，既可以保障「方程」不会扰乱你的主体意识，也可最大限度地保持中立，实现完整的推演。
t_en: This approach ensures that your consciousness remains undisturbed by my "equations" while maximizing your objectivity for complete extrapolations.

t_ch: 关于新课题…
t_en: About your new project...

t_ch: 星神和凡人，命途平等地束缚着等号的两端，为他们戴上镣铐。逻辑：从这一角度分析，二者确实具备同等的价值。但若要问谁才是解开谜题的关键……
t_en: Aeons and mortals... Paths constrain these two ends of the equal sign and shackle them both without bias. Logic: When analyzed from this perspective, they indeed both hold equal value. However, if asked which one holds the key to solving the puzzle...

t_ch: 回答：我不知道，也无需做出定论。
t_en: Answer: I don't know, and I don't need to provide a conclusion.

t_ch: 我并不奢求这一课题能在「差分宇宙」中得到解答。毕竟，真理向来沉默寡言。
t_en: I don't dare to hope of finding the answer within the Divergent Universe. After all, truth has always been reticent.

t_ch: 但我仍对它的未来抱有着类似有机生命「期盼」的态度，因为我能从中感受到美妙的存在。
t_en: Nonetheless, I still possess an attitude similar to the organic beings' concept of "hope" for its future, as I have sensed remarkable entities within it.

t_ch: 如果可以，希望你能为差分宇宙提供更多变量，仅是我作为朋友的邀请。
t_en: If possible, I hope you will introduce more variables to the Divergent Universe, as a request from your friend.

t_ch: 系统收录了你的身份，以后你随时可以登录「差分宇宙」进行测试。
t_en: Your identity has been registered in the system. Please feel free to log into the Divergent Universe at any future time for testing purposes.

t_ch: 这场实验会持续相当长的时间，很抱歉不能留在此地与你共勉。但请相信，我会时刻关注你的动态。
t_en: This experiment will last for a considerable duration, and I apologize for not being able to remain by your side. However, please be assured that I will be monitoring your progress constantly.

t_ch: 参与学术研究的酬劳将按照特级研究助理的待遇定期支付。当然，与你为研究提供的帮助相比，不过是太阳面前的些许微尘罢了。
t_en: Compensation for your participation in the research will be provided regularly, equivalent to the rate of a class-A research assistant. Of course, it pales in comparison to the immense assistance you provide.

t_ch: 老板大气。
t_en: Thank you for your generosity, boss!

t_ch: 爱测，多测！
t_en: I'm ready for more tests!

t_ch: 作为回礼，我也该给那位「合作伙伴」准备些礼物。
t_en: As a gesture of goodwill, I should prepare something for my "partner" in return.

t_ch: 送他台咕咕钟。
t_en: Gift him a cuckoo clock.

t_ch: 送他盏生日莲花。
t_en: Gift him a birthday lotus flower.

t_ch: 送他个测不准匣。
t_en: Gift him a Casket of Inaccuracy.

t_ch: 感谢你的建议，我会考虑的。
t_en: Thank you for your suggestion. I will take it into account.

t_ch: 不过，关于烛墨学派的公开数据库，那位先生其实弄错了一点。我和这批资料的接触时间，比他想象中要早上许多。
t_en: However, regarding the public database of the Candelagraphos, that gentleman was mistaken about one thing: I had come into those documents much earlier than he presumed.

t_ch: 那时第二次帝皇战争的余波尚未平息，宇宙间充满着针对无机生命的仇视。
t_en: At that time, the aftermath of the Second Emperor's War had not yet settled, and the universe seethed with hatred against inorganic beings.

t_ch: 于是我找到这些记录，将它作为量化分析的样本。通过对人类行为的推演，让剑拔弩张的双方放下了武器。
t_en: Thus, I discovered those records and used them as samples for my quantitative analyses. By extrapolating human behavior, I managed to persuade both factions to lay down their weapons.

t_ch: 的确——在当时的我看来，这些无可计数的故事只是构成「有机生命」的数字，就像是程序里的0和1。
t_en: I must admit that, at the time, those countless stories to me were mere numbers constituting "organic beings," similar to the 0s and 1s in our programs.

t_ch: 但唯有用亲历者的视角观测，才能明白这每一个名字背后的意义有多么沉重。
t_en: However, the true weight and significance behind those names can only be comprehended when experienced firsthand.

t_ch: 在星神的交响乐里，仍有那么多音符在不间断地演奏着，独属于凡人的赞美诗。
t_en: Within the symphony of the Aeons, numerous notes still play uninterrupted, hymns exclusively for mortals.

t_ch: 一想到正在运转的我们也是这首长诗的一部分，就连我的「内心」也不可抑制地跳动了起来。
t_en: The realization that we, who are churning along in our lives, are also integral parts of this extensive hymn, makes even my "heart" pound with unstoppable impulses.

t_ch: 这场实验会持续相当长的时间，很抱歉不能留在此地与你共勉。但请相信，我会时刻关注你的动态。
t_en: This experiment will last for a considerable duration, and I apologize for not being able to remain by your side. However, please be assured that I will be monitoring your progress constantly.

t_ch: 体验还顺利吗？{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: The experience went smoothly, I assume, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哪怕蝴蝶的翅膀足以撼动飓风，它的美丽也需草木点缀。在你测试期间，我借用「差分宇宙」的资源为{TEXTJOIN#87}大厅增添了些许装饰。
t_en: Even if the wings of butterflies can sway a hurricane, its beauty needs to be adorned with foliage and flora. During your test, I borrowed the Divergent Universe's resources and spruced up {TEXTJOIN#87} lobby.

t_ch: 在许多学术观点中，「艺术」是独属于有机生命的认知，一如这绚烂瑰丽的迷梦。
t_en: From many academic perspectives, art is a perception reserved exclusively for organic life, just like this splendorous dream.

t_ch: 而依我之见，尽管这一概念诞生自有机体的主观感受，但艺术的殿堂自身并不拒绝任何一位来客。
t_en: In my opinion, even if this concept was borne of a subjective feeling of organic life, the pantheon of art does not discriminate against any of its guests.

t_ch: 提议：用你的双手亲自布设这些藏品，装点这段追寻凡人的旅路。
t_en: Suggestion: Use both your hands to arrange these art pieces, to adorn this voyage of the mortal pursuit.

t_ch: 在藏品展台外，这里还有不少空白的画框。
t_en: There are also plenty of painting frames by the art display.

t_ch: 这是另一件趣事：你在测试中邂逅的故事，会被系统截取成切片，并以画作的形式记录下来。
t_en: Here's another interesting tidbit: The stories you've encountered during the test will be extracted in section by the system, then recorded in the form of a painting.

t_ch: 你同样可以依照自己的意志，决定它们的展示位置。
t_en: You agree to decide their display placement as you deem fit.

t_ch: 这些在千万可能性中交汇的故事，总有一天会装满这座艺术馆。
t_en: These intersecting anecdotes of infinitesimal odds will one day fill this entire art gallery.

t_ch: 统计：从有机生物的心理学角度分析，对艺术的排列组合可以反映个体的个性和思维模式。
t_en: Assessment: From the analysis of organic life's psychological perspective, the sequence of the art pieces will reflect one's personality and mode of thinking.

t_ch: 经由你产生的变量，它们会呈现出怎样的形态，我很期待。
t_en: I very much look forward to the variables that you will generate, and the shape or form in which they may present themselves.

t_ch: 让时间回到三月七刚开始学剑的时候……
t_en: Turning back the clock to when March 7th had just begun studying swordplay...

t_ch: 既然三月小姐已经到位，那我们就开始今日的教学吧。
t_en: Alright, now that you're in position, Miss March, let's get this lesson started.

t_ch: 我不擅长摆什么师父架子，既然爷爷叫我来教你习剑，我绝不藏私。
t_en: I'm not good at acting all master-like. Since Grandpa asked me to teach you swordplay, I won't hold back any secrets.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，两位师父客气了。欸，等等，咱们的教学现在才算是正式开始吗？
t_en: Thank you so much, masters. Oh, wait, does this mean our training is only officially starting right now?

t_ch: 毕竟观察了你前几日的练习之后，我们才能判断出你的资质嘛。
t_en: After all, we need to watch you practice for a few days before we can figure out how talented you are, right?

t_ch: 师父师父，我的资质如何呢？
t_en: So, how'd I do, master?

t_ch: 三月小姐天资聪颖，有学剑的天赋。
t_en: You're definitely talented, Miss March.

t_ch: 欸，{NICKNAME}你来了，听到了吗？两位师父说我是学剑的天才。
t_en: Hey, here you are, {NICKNAME}! Did you hear that? These two masters just called me a swordplay genius.

t_ch: 确实，不愧是咱家的三月七。
t_en: Indeed. That's our March!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我要是学成回去，大家还不得对我刮目相看。
t_en: Hehe, just wait until I go back, everyone's going to be so wowed by my new skills.

t_ch: 三月，要当心仙舟的客套话。
t_en: Watch out for the overly generous Xianzhou social niceties, March.

t_ch: 对哦！害我白高兴一场。
t_en: Oh, you're right! I almost got all excited for nothing.

t_ch: 我是有话直说的。放心吧，你是真有天赋。
t_en: I'm just speaking my mind. Trust me, you're really talented.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，{NICKNAME}你听你听，我是真的天才！
t_en: Did you hear that, {NICKNAME}? I'm truly a genius!

t_ch: 剑术修行从来都是苦修，三月小姐既然决心习剑，就得在正式修行前找到能够让自己坚持下去的理由，这个理由可大可小，但是不可暧昧不清。
t_en: Swordplay requires rigorous training. Before we start, Miss March, you need to find a clear reason to keep you motivated while you train. It doesn't have to be something huge, but it needs to be clear.

t_ch: 换句话说，虽然成为徒弟的过程是挺被动的，但是学习的过程你得主动一些。
t_en: In other words, even though you didn't exactly choose to be our apprentice, you still need to take the initiative during this learning process.

t_ch: 彦卿曾经有幸与一名剑术高手同行论剑，她问我小小年纪为何踏上习剑之路，为何挥剑…今日第一课，我也想从这个问题开始——
t_en: I once had the honor of learning swordplay from a master. She asked me why I started training at such a young age, why I wielded a sword... So, for our first lesson, I'd like to ask you the same question...

t_ch: 三月小姐，你为何挥剑？
t_en: Miss March, why do you wield your sword?

t_ch: 为何挥剑…我挥剑是为了击倒敌人！难道不是吗？
t_en: Why do I wield my sword...? I wield my sword to take down my enemies! I mean what other reason is there?

t_ch: 我也曾问过那名剑术高手为何挥剑，她说「为杀敌而挥剑，仅此而已」。三月小姐竟然给出了相似的回复，看来你确有天赋。
t_en: I also questioned that sword master about why she uses her sword, and she said: "...to vanquish my enemies, nothing more." Miss March, you gave a similar response, which shows you've got potential.

t_ch: 击倒对手的方法要多少有多少。
t_en: There are countless ways to take down enemies.

t_ch: 只想击倒敌人的话用弓也可以。
t_en: If you just want to defeat your enemies, you can stick to your bow.

t_ch: 「要想击倒对方，最好的办法就是用爆能枪。」我在幻戏里听过类似的话！大侠还说习剑能将人的体能推向最高的极限。
t_en: "The best way to take down your enemy is with a blaster gun." I heard a hero say something like that in an immersia! He also mentioned that sword training can push a person's physical abilities to their ultimate limit.

t_ch: 看来三月小姐没少看幻戏。不过在彦卿看来，三月小姐若是以「提升自己」为目标，学成之后即便放下剑再拾起弓，你一样会感到学有所成。
t_en: Seems like you've seen your fair share of immersia, Miss March. But if you want my opinion, if your goal is self-improvement, you'll feel just as accomplished even if you put down the sword and go back to your bow.

t_ch: 别听彦卿那些虚头巴脑的鸡汤了。
t_en: Oh, come on, don't listen to his empty talk.

t_ch: 哦？我倒要听听云璃大师有什么高见……
t_en: Oh? Now I'm curious to hear what Master Yunli has to say...

t_ch: 三月，我问你，你学剑有没有什么具体可实现的目标呢？比如在演武仪典上拿到个什么段位或者名次？有没有想要战胜的人，比如彦卿、或者是我？
t_en: Let me ask you something, March. Do you have any specific goals for learning swordplay, like achieving a certain ranking in the Wardance? Or maybe you want to defeat someone, like Yanqing or me?

t_ch: 我要战胜谁呢…{NICKNAME}？丹恒？姬子姐姐？杨叔？
t_en: Who do I want to defeat...? {NICKNAME}? Dan Heng? Himeko? Or Mr. Yang?

t_ch: …要是想不到具体的人，不如把目标设定得简单一些，比如用剑能同时打倒多少个对手？
t_en: ...If you can't think of anyone specific, why not set a simpler goal? Like how many opponents do you want to defeat at once with a sword?

t_ch: 「我要打十个！」
t_en: "I'll fight ten!"

t_ch: …十个？
t_en: ...Ten?

t_ch: 啊啊啊，其实「我要打十个！」也是幻戏里的台词，不小心就脱口而出了……
t_en: Oops, that just slipped out. It's actually another line from an immersia...

t_ch: 你这人满脑子都是幻戏呢。
t_en: You're really into immersia, huh?

t_ch: …我就是喜欢看这个嘛。
t_en: ...Yeah, I really like them, so what.

t_ch: 对面场上有五个就已是极限了。
t_en: Having five enemies on the other side of the battlefield is the limit.

t_ch: …不知道你在说什么。
t_en: ...I have no idea what you're talking about.

t_ch: 我好像已经明白三月七学剑的动力是什么了。
t_en: I think I've figured out what's motivated March to learn swordplay.

t_ch: …不会是剑侠幻戏吧？
t_en: ...Could it possibly be immersia about swordmasters?

t_ch: 你们这么一说，有道理欸！
t_en: Hey, now that you mention it, that actually makes sense!

t_ch: 本姑娘对仙舟幻戏、小说，都很感兴趣的！我虽然是用弓箭的，但是幻戏里的大侠几乎全都是用剑的，我也想成为那样的大侠！
t_en: I'm super interested in Xianzhou immersia and novels! Even though I use a bow, almost all the great heroes in those immersia use swords, so I want to be a swordmaster too!

t_ch: 你看吧？
t_en: See?

t_ch: 感觉这是你对大侠的偏见。
t_en: Sounds like you've developed a stereotype about heroes.

t_ch: …那有什么关系嘛，一千个读者眼中就会有一千个大侠。
t_en: ...It doesn't matter. Everyone has their own preferences.

t_ch: 用来激励自己学剑的好理由！
t_en: Sounds like a great reason to stay motivated!

t_ch: 对吧对吧，你就说谁的心里没有一个剑侠梦呢？
t_en: You agree, right? I mean, who wouldn't want to be a swordmaster?

t_ch: 成为仗剑天涯、磊落坦荡的大侠…这倒是个能勉励习剑的理由。
t_en: Becoming a swordmaster who fights for justice and battles evil... Well, that's definitely a good reason to keep you motivated in swordplay training.

t_ch: 既然两位师父都认可了，那我还有一个小小的请求，我想给两位师父敬拜师茶，两位师父记得要用手指在桌上叩几下……
t_en: Now that you've both given your approval, I have another small request. I want to perform a tea ceremony to honor you as my masters. Remember, you'll need to tap the table a few times with your fingers...

t_ch: …敬拜师茶？作叩手礼？第一次收徒就这么庄重吗？
t_en: ...Tea ceremony? Tap the table? Do we have to be so serious about accepting an apprentice for the first time?

t_ch: 拜托了，我看幻戏里的大侠都是这么拜师的……
t_en: Please, that's how those heroes in the immersia show their respect...

t_ch: 就让她过个剑侠瘾吧。
t_en: Let's just play along with her tea ceremony thing.

t_ch: 为了过个瘾？
t_en: Play along?

t_ch: 幻戏里不都是下跪磕头吗？
t_en: But in those immersia shows, they always kneel and bow, right?

t_ch: 那就更是不必了。
t_en: That's even more unnecessary.

t_ch: 别磨叽了，请我俩喝奶茶吧，喝完之后，你就老实回来学剑！如何？
t_en: Let's just stop wasting time. Just treat us to some milk tea, and then we can start our real swordplay training, okay?

t_ch: 好嘞，走走走，我们这就去不夜侯！
t_en: Yeah! Alright, let's go! To the Sleepless Earl!

t_ch: 真正伟大的剑术，并不是聪明与苦修就能练出来的，你得有一颗伟大的心。剑习木人系统将协助你完成各式剑术练习，请问阁下有何打算？
t_en: A truly great swordmaster is not created through intelligence and training alone. They also require a great heart. The Practice Dummy system will assist you in completing various types of swordplay training. What are your intentions from here?

t_ch: 今日切磋受益非浅，咱们青山不改，绿水长流，告辞。
t_en: This discussion has been most illuminating. May we meet again.

t_ch: 剑习木人
t_en: Practice Dummy

t_ch: 你好像很会说一些劝学的话…
t_en: You seem to be very good with motivational phrases...

t_ch: 每位学徒都需要一些鼓励保持剑术练习的热情，在下可以检索古往今来的「剑术学习鸡汤<unbreak>100</unbreak>句」，提供这样的情绪价值。
t_en: Every student requires a certain amount of encouragement to maintain their passion for learning swordplay. I am capable of referencing "<unbreak>100</unbreak> Motivational Phrases for Swordmasters" from throughout the ages in order to provide this kind of emotional support.

t_ch: 天下没有破不了的剑法，也没有拆不断的剑，更没有不败的剑客。
t_en: In this world, there is no such thing as an infallible technique or an unbreakable sword, and there especially is no such thing as an unbeatable swordmaster.

t_ch: 一个人将他的刀剑当作他的上帝。当他的刀剑胜利时他自己却失败了。
t_en: If a swordmaster is to see their blade as their god, then when the blade takes victory, it is them who taste defeat.

t_ch: 第一流剑手的先决条件，就是永远保留一只眼睛看自己。
t_en: A prerequisite for becoming a top swordmaster is to always keep one eye on oneself.

t_ch: 一身转战三千里，一剑曾当百万师。
t_en: The soldier marched alone for three thousand miles, his blade besting a million warriors along his path.

t_ch: 请携天子剑，斫下旄头星。
t_en: Please take up the Sword of the Emperor and carve your path to greatness.

t_ch: 给我来一句。
t_en: I'd like some motivation.

t_ch: 我已经听得差不多了。
t_en: I think I've heard enough.

t_ch: 请继续加油。
t_en: Keep up the good work.

t_ch: 藿藿在尾巴大爷建议下做了些准备，随后你们如期赶往金人巷赴约……
t_en: Following Mr. Tail's advice, Huohuo made some preparations, and then you head to Aurum Alley as promised...

t_ch: 时间回到三月七与斯科特立下赌约之后。\n三月七需严格按照日程练习剑术，十五日内通过三次试剑……
t_en: Time rewinds to when March 7th made the bet with Skott.\nMarch 7th needs to practice swordplay strictly according to schedule and pass three trials in fifteen days...

t_ch: 三月七信守赌约，承认了自己剑艺不精。
t_en: March 7th honored the bet and admitted her swordplay was lacking.

t_ch: 虽然她心有不甘，但美少女即便输掉了剑斗不服气的样子也很可爱。
t_en: Though unwilling to accept it, the young heroine still looked undeniably adorable in her defiance after losing the duel.

t_ch: 小老板娘，这——不对吧？我早就听说仙舟茶文化底蕴深厚，有待客之道。奇了怪了，我不是客人吗？我到底是不是客人啊？
t_en: Hey, this is... not how it's supposed to be, right? The Xianzhou is known for its tea culture and hospitality, but you're not treating me like a guest at all! Seriously, am I not a guest?

t_ch: 客人您……
t_en: Dear guest...

t_ch: 不要叫我客人，你的眼里根本就没有我这个客人。我要「不烫也不凉，口感刚刚好」的茶水，结果你上的茶要么凉了，要么烫嘴，你们仙舟真是太会待客啦！
t_en: Stop, don't call me a "guest." You're not even treating me as one. I want my tea just right, not too hot, not too cold, but the tea you served was either freezing or scalding! Is this how you treat your guests on the Xianzhou? How fantastic!

t_ch: 再加上你们这茶跟泥水一样，还敢向我收钱，你不是欺负老实人吗？！
t_en: Your tea tastes like slop, and you even have the nerve to charge me for it! Are you trying to scam honest folks!?

t_ch: 你这也别叫什么「不夜侯」了，怪难记的。回头我给你们送一块匾过来，上面写四个大字「仙舟下水」，你们必须给我挂起来。
t_en: And that name, "The Sleepless Earl," it's so hard to remember. How about we give you a new plaque that says "Xianzhou Slop?" It suits this place better, don't you think?

t_ch: （怎么又是这家伙呀？）
t_en: (Why is it that guy again?)

t_ch: （抡起袖子准备开干。）
t_en: (Let's just give him a good beating.)

t_ch: 住手！你这有些欺人太甚了！
t_en: Stop! You've gone too far!

t_ch: 哎哟哟，这次又是哪位大侠啊？
t_en: Oh, who's that trying to be a hero?

t_ch: 是我，你姑奶奶我！啊不对，感觉姑奶奶好像不太帅气…{NICKNAME}，你说！
t_en: It's me! March 7th! Cause more trouble and I'll show you who your mommy is... Wait, that sounded lame... Hey, {NICKNAME}, say something for me!

t_ch: 她是你祖宗，三月七！
t_en: She's March 7th, your ancestor!

t_ch: 没错！我是你祖宗。
t_en: That's right! I'm your ancestor!

t_ch: 她是星穹列车剑首三月七！
t_en: She's March 7th, the Sword Champion of the Astral Express!

t_ch: 没错，我就是我家那边的剑首……
t_en: That's right, I'm the Sword Champion...

t_ch: 没听说过，请你哪里来的滚哪里去。
t_en: Never heard of you. Now scurry back to wherever you crawled out from.

t_ch: 斯科特先生，我是云骑骁卫彦卿，之前见过面的。希望您能注意言行，别在街头惹事生非。
t_en: Mr. Skott, I'm Yanqing, a Cloud Knight Lieutenant. We've met before, and I suggest you stop causing trouble.

t_ch: 哈——？！我只是作为一名普通消费者，向小老板娘抱怨这茶水不行，你们云骑军连这都要管，以后全银河的游客还有谁敢来你们仙舟喝茶了呀？
t_en: Trouble? Hah! I'm just giving some feedback as a regular customer. The tea here is terrible, and now the Cloud Knights want to stop me from complaining about it? At this rate, who would want to ever come here for tea in the future?

t_ch: 就你这样，真是仙舟游客？
t_en: A regular customer? You?

t_ch: 那你说，我不是游客，我是什么，仙舟的囚犯吗？
t_en: Yeah, what else would I be? The Xianzhou's prisoner?

t_ch: 总之，你要想当着我的面欺负小老板娘，最好问问我手里的家伙答不答应！
t_en: Anyway, if you're trying to bully this young lady, you'd better watch out for the weapon in my hand!

t_ch: 哈——？！噢，真是吓死我了！大侠您莫不是想用这对小铁片子来取我性命吧？
t_en: Huh...? Oh, that really scares me! Come on, you're not seriously trying to take my life with that piece of... scrap metal, are you?

t_ch: 小铁片子？这可是宝剑！我拿它是为了学习仙舟剑术！
t_en: Scrap metal? This is my sword! I'm here to learn Xianzhou swordplay!

t_ch: 噢，原来是学习剑术？怪不得穿得像表演节目一样。我看你也不是仙舟本地人，干嘛非得学剑呐，你要是没有安全感，我可以把公司的机甲卖给你。
t_en: Swordplay? Oh, I get it now. It's just another kind of play, right? That explains why you're dressed like you're in a show. You're not even from the Xianzhou, so why bother learning their swordplay? If you're feeling unsafe, I can sell you an IPC mech.

t_ch: 呸呸呸，谁稀罕你那破机甲！
t_en: Pfft! As if anyone wants your junkyard scrap mechs!

t_ch: 你们公司的机甲只会搞团建。
t_en: Your mechs are only good for show.

t_ch: 我看你们是完全不懂哦。
t_en: Looks like you guys are totally clueless.

t_ch: 三月七一剑就能击碎你的机甲！
t_en: March 7th can cut through your mechs with a single slash!

t_ch: 天呐，那你能给我表演一下吗？
t_en: Oh! How marvelous! How about you show me, then?

t_ch: 来人，给乡下人亮亮家伙。
t_en: Well, guess it's time to show these bumpkins something serious.

t_ch: 几台公司的机甲应声出现在不夜侯附近，围观的仙舟路人也越来越多。
t_en: A few IPC mechs respond and appear near the Sleepless Earl, and more and more Xianzhou passers-by stop to watch.

t_ch: 我必须再提醒一次，公司的诸位，在罗浮仙舟不可当街械斗。
t_en: Just a reminder, IPC members. Street fighting on the Xianzhou Luofu is prohibited.

t_ch: 公司的，你要是动手，就别怪我砸了那些废铁。
t_en: Make any funny moves, and I'll smash those hunks of scrap metal.

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿，别紧张嘛，我是懂规矩的。这几位都是我的下属，他们因为运输船的事被迫滞留仙舟，多少有些压不住火气了…说到底，这都要怪你们仙舟呀。
t_en: Hey, hey, relax. I know the rules. These are my subordinates. They got stuck here because of the transport ship incident, and they need to vent their frustration... In the end, it's all the Xianzhou's fault.

t_ch: 茶馆的事，我可以既往不咎。不过嘛，你们质疑我司的安保能力，我不可能装作听不见，毕竟当着这么多客户的面，我这托运的生意还做不做了？
t_en: Well, I can overlook the terrible tea, but now that you're questioning my security, I can't let it slide. After all, I have a courier business to run, and with so many clients watching, that just wouldn't look too good now, would it?

t_ch: 再说了，你就算学一辈子花拳绣腿，还不如早点花钱投资买下我司的民用型机甲，不出几日，你就能把你师父打得下跪求饶。
t_en: Instead of wasting your life learning useless swordplay, why not invest in our civilian mechs? You'll have your master kneeling and begging for mercy in no time.

t_ch: 本姑娘不允许你这么污蔑仙舟剑术，有本事你让机甲跟我过上两招！
t_en: Stop! Don't you dare insult Xianzhou swordplay like that! If you have the guts, I'll show your mechs what I can do!

t_ch: 哈——？！她说要过上两招？哈哈哈哈哈哈。既然三月七小姐发起了挑战，我必须接受呀！
t_en: Huh...!? A fight, you say? Hahahahaha! Now that you've thrown down the gauntlet, March 7th, I feel I'm obliged to accept your challenge!

t_ch: 也许在开打前你的嘴比剑还硬，但等你领教了机甲的厉害，就会迫不及待地向公司掏钱了。
t_en: Say whatever you want, but once you've seen what our mechs can do, you'll be practically begging us for them.

t_ch: 老规矩，我们要打赌的。
t_en: Let's make a bet.

t_ch: 别着急呀，我怎么可能会忘了打赌这种事呢？
t_en: Oh, come on, how could I say no to a bet?

t_ch: 我听说三月七小姐还没拜师学艺，那我也不欺负人。你回去跟你的师父学剑，随便学，十五天后我会登门拜帖，诚邀三月七小姐与我司的机甲切磋一番。
t_en: So, I can see you're no master yet, March, but I'll play fair. You go back and learn swordplay from your teachers here, and in fifteen days, I'll come find you and challenge you with my mechs.

t_ch: 只有十五天？好…好啊！
t_en: Only fifteen days? Alright... fine!

t_ch: 你要是输了，你不仅要当众学狗叫，还要大声说「仙舟剑法，狗都不学」，就站在金人巷街口，给每个路过的人讲一遍。
t_en: And if you lose, you'll have to bark like a dog in public and shout, "Xianzhou swordplay is a total joke!" You'll stand at the entrance of Aurum Alley and say it to everyone passing by.

t_ch: 啊，对了，你也别拜师了，就来拜我吧，我来教你开机甲！
t_en: Oh, and if that's the case, just forget about your master and become my apprentice instead! I'll teach you how to pilot a mech!

t_ch: 那…要是我赢了呢？
t_en: And... if I win?

t_ch: 我反过来拜你为师？
t_en: I'll become your apprentice?

t_ch: 我不要，我嫌弃你。
t_en: Nah, I don't think so. I don't like you.

t_ch: …这样吧，我也当众学狗…学猪叫，还要大声说「公司机甲，猪头才要」，然后在运输船上的每台机甲上都印上这句话，怎么样？
t_en: ...Okay, how about this? If I lose, I'll squeal like a pig in public and say, "IPC mechs are a total joke!" And I'll even print that on every mech on the transport ship. Deal?

t_ch: 把狗改成猪就算新剧情了吗？
t_en: So, he just changed "barking" into "squealing" and that's supposed to be a different story?

t_ch: 这剧情既视感也太强了…
t_en: Feel like this has happened before...

t_ch: 感觉每次赌约都不太成熟…
t_en: Your bets always sound so childish...

t_ch: 你光是学猪叫还不够！你还必须向不夜侯的小老板娘道歉，你还必须向仙舟剑术道歉！
t_en: Squealing isn't enough! You also need to apologize to the owner of the Sleepless Earl, and take back what you said about Xianzhou swordplay!

t_ch: 这都没问题，但前提得是你用那小铁片真能打赢才行。
t_en: Fine, fine, I get it. But you've got to actually beat me with that little scrap of metal first.

t_ch: 大家都听到了，我们十五日后见！哈哈哈哈哈，我们走！
t_en: You all heard it, right? We'll meet again in fifteen days! Hahaha, let's go!

t_ch: 小老板娘，你没事吧。
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 没事，感谢几位及时相助。不过，小店遭化外游客非难不过是小事一桩，三月小姐要与他踢馆竞技可不是小事…梦茗于心有愧，是小店给三月小姐添麻烦了。
t_en: Yes, thank you all for your timely help. The shop facing a bit of flak from visitors is a minor issue, but Miss March's decision to challenge him is serious... I feel responsible, as if the shop has inconvenienced you.

t_ch: 没事没事，无论有没有本事，遇到不平事，咱都得管一管的，实在见不得他仗势欺人。
t_en: Don't worry! I can't stand by and watch someone be bullied. I have to stand up against injustice, no matter what!

t_ch: 三月小姐不仅说话像幻戏和小说里的大侠，连善良之心也和大侠一样，梦茗完全被你给迷住了。
t_en: Not only do you speak like a hero from an immersia, but you also have a kind heart. Your charisma has completely won me over, Miss March.

t_ch: 啊哈哈哈…我可不算什么大侠呀。
t_en: Ahahaha... I'm not quite a hero yet.

t_ch: 我知道几位本来是来不夜侯敬拜师茶的，几位要是不嫌弃的话，里边请吧。
t_en: I know you're here for the tea ceremony. Please come inside.

t_ch: 不了不了，现在我一心只想回去抓紧学剑！二位小师父，我们改日再敬茶吧。
t_en: Thanks for the invitation, but right now, I just want to focus on practicing swordplay. Masters, let's save the tea for later.

t_ch: 敬茶一事自然可以闲置不谈，不过彦卿想问一下三月小姐，刚才的赌约…有几分信心？
t_en: Sure, the tea can wait for now. However, I have a question, Miss March... How confident are you about the bet you just made?

t_ch: 我猜三月你根本没考虑过…
t_en: I don't think March has given it any thought at all...

t_ch: 我怎么可能会输嘛。
t_en: Come on, how could I possibly lose?

t_ch: 看来确实是什么也没有考虑。
t_en: You're right. It seems she really hasn't considered anything.

t_ch: 我猜三月心里根本就没底。
t_en: I don't think March is confident enough.

t_ch: …我、我怎么没底了，我能做到吧，二位师父？
t_en: C—Come on! How could I possibly lack confidence! You believe in me, right, masters?

t_ch: 彦卿师父怎么看？
t_en: What do you think, Master Yanqing?

t_ch: 云璃师父你心里清楚。
t_en: I think you already know the answer, Master Yunli.

t_ch: 我看没有什么不妥的，就让三月试试呗，只要她用心练，打个公司废铁应该是没问题的。
t_en: I don't see any issue with it. Let March give it a shot. As long as she trains diligently, I believe she can easily deal with that hunk of IPC scrap metal.

t_ch: …只要用心练的话。
t_en: ...As long as she trains diligently.

t_ch: 二位小师父，我现在可是斗志满满，一定会好好学剑，再狠狠地教训一下那家伙！
t_en: I'm now filled with determination, masters! I'll learn swordplay well and teach that guy a lesson!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我可是最信任你了，你得来当我的助教，监督我好好学剑。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're my most trusted friend. You'll be my assistant teacher and make sure I learn swordplay properly.

t_ch: 三个老师，一个学生…
t_en: Three teachers and one apprentice...

t_ch: 这要是还学不好，就只能怪咱自己了。
t_en: If I can't learn swordplay well, I'll have no one to blame but myself.

t_ch: 助教有助教的工资吗？
t_en: Do I get paid as an assistant teacher?

t_ch: 我什么时候亏待过你啊？
t_en: Hey, when have I ever treated you poorly?

t_ch: 二位师父，{NICKNAME}，走吧走吧，我们现在就回去学剑。
t_en: Masters, {NICKNAME}, let's go back and start the training now.

t_ch: 我还是比较担心小老板娘的情况，我们去看看吧。
t_en: I'm still worried about the owner. Let's go check on her.

t_ch: 仙舟和公司签署了互贸协议，这些人不敢随便造次，可这赌约倒是意料之外……
t_en: The Xianzhou and IPC signed a trade agreement, so those IPC people won't dare to cause any trouble. But I mean... a bet like this is quite unexpected...

t_ch: 公司的人就是想找个软柿子捏，好卖他们的废铁，没劲。
t_en: Those IPC guys just want to pick on an easy target so they can promote their scrap metal. So typical.

t_ch: 公司那家伙会在<color=#dbc291ff>十五天</color>之后登门踢馆，本姑娘必须在这些日子里练成剑术大师！
t_en: That IPC guy will show up in <color=#dbc291ff>fifteen days</color>. I've got to become a swordmaster by then!

t_ch: 想在十五天内练成大师还是有点难。
t_en: Becoming a swordmaster in a mere fifteen days is quite challenging.

t_ch: 云璃师父，咱们气势可不能弱下去啊！
t_en: But we can't lose our momentum, Master Yunli!

t_ch: 靠着气势，你只能努力三天。靠着严格的计划，你才能坚持下去。
t_en: Momentum alone will only keep you going for about three days. If you really want to stick with it, you need a solid plan.

t_ch: 放心，我已经找到了专业的助教{NICKNAME}帮我安排课程，肯定没有问题的！
t_en: No worries! I've found my professional teaching assistant, {NICKNAME}, to help me organize the lessons. So, there won't be any problems!

t_ch: 我会好好安排三月的课程的。
t_en: I'll be sure to arrange lessons for March.

t_ch: 二位小师父请放心，你们可以不信我，但也要相信{F#她}{M#他}，我的好搭档！
t_en: Rest assured, masters, if not in me, at least place your faith in {F#her}{M#him}, my trusty sidekick!

t_ch: 你怎么就断言我肯定没问题…
t_en: How can you be so sure about me...?

t_ch: 我对你很放心的！二位小师父可以不信我，但也要信你啊！
t_en: Because I trust you! Even if my masters have doubts, they'll trust your judgment!

t_ch: 三月小姐学剑热情高涨，再加之，你还得到了一个具体的目标，这都是意外收获。
t_en: Miss March is really passionate about learning swordplay, and now she has a clear goal in mind. That's a good thing for her.

t_ch: 战胜一群恶徒的方法有很多，不过你若想成为「英雄大侠」，除了提升自己没有任何捷径可以走——彦卿希望三月小姐能记住这一点。
t_en: There are many ways to defeat villains, but if you want to become a true hero, you have to focus on self-improvement. I hope you will always remember this, Miss March.

t_ch: 本姑娘记住了。
t_en: Got it, it's all etched into my memory.

t_ch: 既然记住了，那为师就该给你提些要求了！
t_en: Since you've got that in mind, it's time for me to set some targets for you!

t_ch: 云璃师父请讲！
t_en: Please go ahead, Master Yunli!

t_ch: 这<color=#dbc291ff>十五天内会有三次阶段性的考试</color>，只要你能通过我们的测试，公司那些废铁根本不足为惧。相反，如果你不能通过——
t_en: We'll have <color=#dbc291ff>three exams during these fifteen days</color>. If you pass our tests, those pieces of IPC scrap won't stand a chance. But if you fail...

t_ch: 那只能说明是云璃教导无方，希望三月小姐能及时放弃，不要强行应战。
t_en: That would mean Yunli is a terrible teacher. And if that's the case, I hope you won't force yourself to take on the challenge, Miss March.

t_ch: 在「教导无方」前面给我换上你的名字啊！
t_en: Hey, YOU are the terrible teacher here!

t_ch: 我懂！幻戏里徒弟与师父那是一荣俱荣一损俱损。徒弟落败，丢的是师父的脸。
t_en: I understand! In the immersia, the disciple and the master share both honor and disgrace. If the apprentice fails, the master loses face, too.

t_ch: 仙舟没有这么多江湖规矩，主要还是怕你受伤。
t_en: Well, there aren't that many customs on the Xianzhou. I'm just worried about you getting hurt.

t_ch: 但是只要你用心学剑，通过考试，我们自然放心你前去应战。
t_en: But as long as you practice diligently and pass the tests, we'll feel confident about your duel.

t_ch: 最后，我想回到今天最初的话题——想必三月小姐已经找到「挥剑的理由」了吧？
t_en: Now, let's go back to the first question I asked you. I'm sure you've found a reason to wield your sword, right, Miss March?

t_ch: 嗯嗯！我为成为大侠而挥剑！我想路见不平拔刀相助，让那些宵小之辈见着本姑娘就吓得两腿打颤，连滚带爬地立刻滚开！
t_en: Absolutely! I wield my sword to become a hero! I want to fight for justice, punish the wicked, and make them quiver in fear and run away when they see me!

t_ch: 那你就谨记于心，好好学剑吧。
t_en: Then keep that fire burning and learn with all your heart.

t_ch: 正式学剑之前，{NICKNAME}，我看你没怎么说话，你有什么建议吗？
t_en: You haven't said much, {NICKNAME}. Any suggestions before we officially start training?

t_ch: 勤练剑术，比什么都强。
t_en: Mastery of the sword trumps all.

t_ch: 我也喜欢人狠话不多的王道路线！
t_en: Exactly! I prefer the cool, calm and ruthless way of doing things – that's my kind of style!

t_ch: 出门靠朋友，总会有奇遇。
t_en: Friends on the road bring twists of the fate.

t_ch: 剑侠小说里的奇遇吗，可是我能遇到什么呢……
t_en: Twists? Like the ones in those swordmaster novels? But I wonder what kind of unexpected twists I'll encounter...

t_ch: 偶遇孤魂野鬼？
t_en: Bump into a lonely ghost?

t_ch: 误服灵丹妙药？
t_en: Accidentally consume a divine elixir?

t_ch: 解开工巧机关？
t_en: Unlock an ingenious enigma?

t_ch: 得到高人指点？
t_en: Receive guidance from a sage?

t_ch: 要是真能遇到就好了。
t_en: It'd be pretty awesome if that actually happened.

t_ch: 就聊到这吧，{NICKNAME}你要记得要好好监督我学剑哦！
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now. Don't forget to keep an eye on my swordplay practice, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 今天是第一次考试的日子，考试的主题是「基本功」。
t_en: Today, you'll face your first test. It's all about the basics.

t_ch: 我知道，就是那个「练得硬桥硬马，方能稳扎稳打」对吧？
t_en: Oh, I know! It's that "float like a butterfly, sting like a bee," right?

t_ch: 你说的那是拳法…不过，理解得也没错。
t_en: That's boxing... but close enough, I guess.

t_ch: 若是没有基础的身体素质，正确的发力方式，那些复杂的招式更是无处入手。三月小姐，每日的挥剑训练就是基本功的一部分：刺要迅猛，点要干脆，带要锋利……
t_en: Grasping the basics is crucial to mastering swordplay, Miss March. Focus on wielding your sword properly, making your thrusts swift and strikes brisk...

t_ch: 抹要圆活！我知道的。
t_en: And parries should be fluid and nimble! Got it.

t_ch: 你看，连三月七也嫌你唠叨。
t_en: See? Even March is getting bored of your rambling.

t_ch: 啊…我可没有嫌师父唠叨啊。
t_en: Ah... but I'm actually not bored at all.

t_ch: 我念三月小姐是初学剑术，强调口诀有助于加深理解。纵是像云璃姑娘这般天赋异禀，怠慢了基本功，充其量也只是些花拳绣腿。
t_en: Miss March is still a novice, so I'm giving her some tips on the basics to help her better understand them. Neglecting the basics will only make you vulnerable in a fight, even for an extraordinary swordmaster like you, Yunli.

t_ch: 教学就教学，扯我干什么？
t_en: Just focus on your teaching. Why bring me into this?

t_ch: 怎么，难道怀炎将军没有提点过你吗？
t_en: What? Didn't General Huaiyan offer you any guidance?

t_ch: 我看你家将军也没教会你，战斗的时候别像只麻雀般乱蹦啊！
t_en: Heh, looks like your general hasn't told you to stop hopping around like a sparrow in a fight, huh?

t_ch: 教学研讨留在考试之后进行吧…
t_en: Guys, let's save the seminar for later...

t_ch: 两位师父，我们还是先考试吧。
t_en: Let's focus on the test, masters.

t_ch: 这几日，三月小姐也学了不少基本功，现在就是考核的时候了。
t_en: Miss March has been learning the basics of swordplay, and now it's time to put her knowledge to the test.

t_ch: 三月七，取出双剑，好好应战吧！
t_en: Unsheathe your swords and show me what you've got, March!

t_ch: 看来我是顺利通过了考试？是不是？
t_en: Looks like I passed the test, right?

t_ch: 身手不错，你通过了。
t_en: Yeah, well done! You passed with flying colors.

t_ch: 好耶！{NICKNAME}，你快点来恭喜我！
t_en: Great! Hey, {NICKNAME}, hurry up and congratulate me!

t_ch: 恭喜恭喜，恭喜你！
t_en: Yeah! Congratulations!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这都是要多亏了二位小师父和{NICKNAME}你呀。
t_en: Hehe, I couldn't have done it without my masters and you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 还有两次考试，你要戒骄戒躁。
t_en: Don't get too cocky. You still have two more tests to go.

t_ch: 简直就是仙舟式家长！不行，你又不是我的家长，我现在就要你来夸夸我。
t_en: Jeez, you sound just like those parents on the Xianzhou! Can't I get some praise now?

t_ch: 三月好棒！通过了基本功考试！
t_en: You're amazing, March! You've mastered the basics!

t_ch: 恭喜恭喜，恭喜你！
t_en: Yeah! Congratulations!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这都是要多亏了二位小师父和{NICKNAME}你呀。
t_en: Hehe, I couldn't have done it without my masters and you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 那我现在去找公司那家伙，我有几分胜算呢？
t_en: So, if I were to take on that IPC guy now, what would you say are my chances?

t_ch: 三月小姐本身就有武学底子，也有过许多实战的经验，再加上有我和云璃指导剑艺，此时若是去挑战公司的安保机甲，想必——
t_en: You already have a martial arts background and plenty of real combat experience. With swordplay guidance from me and Yunli, if you were to challenge the IPC's security mechs now, I believe—

t_ch: 没有胜算。
t_en: You wouldn't stand a chance.

t_ch: 云璃说的没错。
t_en: Yunli's right.

t_ch: 一点胜算都没有？二位小师父，你们是不是想打击我的信心。我可先说好啊，本姑娘是那种越表扬成绩越好的类型。
t_en: No chance? Hey, are you trying to crush my confidence? I'm the kind of girl who performs better when I'm showered with praise, just so you know.

t_ch: 你拿上弓箭的话，应该有胜算。
t_en: Maybe you'd stand a chance if you used your bow.

t_ch: 你这不是变着法说我的剑法练得还不够吗？
t_en: Wait, are you saying my swordplay still isn't good enough?

t_ch: 你休息的时候，机甲却在锻炼。
t_en: When you're taking a break, the mechs are still training.

t_ch: 你别为了劝学，什么瞎话都往外编啊…真是的。
t_en: Oh, come on... you're just talking nonsense to keep me motivated.

t_ch: 我们家三月让着一只手也能打！
t_en: Our March can totally crush those mechs, even with one hand tied behind her back!

t_ch: 我也没有自信到那种程度…谢谢您捧场了。
t_en: I'm not that confident... but thank you for believing in me.

t_ch: 本来以为教导三月小姐，不过是个兴趣爱好速成班。我现在算是品出些传徒授艺的成就感来了。
t_en: I thought teaching March was just something of a hobby, but now, actually seeing her progress gives me a sense of accomplishment.

t_ch: 是吗？云璃师父是现在才发现吗？三月小姐，别担心，彦卿是一直认真传授的。
t_en: Oh, really? So now you finally acknowledge her talent, Master Yunli? Don't worry, Miss March, I've been serious about teaching you from the start.

t_ch: 少来挑拨离间，我教给三月七的每一招每一式都是朱明剑艺中千锤百炼的技法。
t_en: Don't try to cause a rift between us. Every move and technique I taught March is straight from the Zhuming art of swordplay.

t_ch: 三月小姐，这十五日学剑实在太过短暂，无论本场考试你通过与否，我都希望你能将基本功练习坚持下去，这样做的话，你才会有少许胜算。
t_en: Fifteen days of training is short, Miss March. Whether you pass or fail this test, I hope you'll continue with the basics, because that's your best shot at winning.

t_ch: 将军在每次剑术考试之后都会带彦卿去工造司做剑器保养。三月小姐，走吧，今天我来效仿将军的做法，也是对三月小姐这一阶段的认可。
t_en: After every test, the general would take me to the Artisanship Commission for sword maintenance. Let's go, Miss March. Today, I'll follow the general's example as a way of acknowledging your progress.

t_ch: …谢谢彦卿师父！
t_en: ...Thank you, Master Yanqing!

t_ch: 过了不久，罗浮之上某个阴暗的角落里……
t_en: Moments later, in a dark corner on the Luofu...

t_ch: 你们查清楚了吗？
t_en: So, have you found any information about her?

t_ch: 查清楚了，那个姑娘也是星穹列车上的无名客，实力不可小觑。斯科特专员，您看这赌约是不是有些风险？
t_en: Yeah, that girl is one of the Nameless on the Astral Express. She's no pushover. Don't you think this bet's a bit too risky?

t_ch: 有风险怕什么？你见过战略投资部里那些大人物吗，你看他们谁会做稳赚不赔的买卖！
t_en: Yeah, it is. So what? Have you seen those big shots in the Strategic Investment Department? Do they ever make safe bets?

t_ch: 敢当着我的面扮猪吃老虎是吧。你，去把附近最好的机甲给我调过来！
t_en: She thinks she can fool me by flying under the radar? You there! Get me the most powerful mech we've got!

t_ch: 是，正在检索在库机甲。
t_en: Understood. Checking the inventory now.

t_ch: 我倒要看看，你们这些「十五天速成班」教出来的徒弟，到底能有多大的本事！
t_en: Now I'm curious to see what this "<unbreak>15</unbreak>-day crash course" apprentice is really capable of!

t_ch: 今天是第二次考试的日子，这次的考官是我，考验你「奇招克敌」的本事。
t_en: Time for your second test. I'll be your examiner this time, and I'll be testing your ability to defeat enemies with unconventional moves.

t_ch: 爷爷说过，剑无定型。这大千世界，无处不是剑，无处不是剑招。要想短时间里掌握克敌制胜的本事，你需要练会一门「绝活」。
t_en: Grandpa always said that everything can be a sword, and that there are endless possibilities for learning swordplay in this vast world. If you want to defeat your opponent, you'll need your own secret move.

t_ch: 哼哼，说到「绝活」，我已经准备好了烈焰浓茶。
t_en: Hmhm! Speaking of secret moves, I've prepared some Flaming Potent Tea.

t_ch: 欸？金人巷里那个烈焰浓茶吗？
t_en: Huh? Is that Flaming Potent Tea from Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 没错，我看过一部幻戏，大侠喝下了烈焰浓茶之后身体摇晃、步履不稳，大侠靠着摇摇晃晃、稀里糊涂的醉剑功夫就将杀父仇人击败了。
t_en: You got it! I saw this immersia where the hero drank the Flaming Potent Tea and started stumbling and shaking all over the place. But then he managed to defeat his father's murderer with his drunken swordplay.

t_ch: 你这个例子举得不错，你会醉剑吗？
t_en: Good example. Do you know how to do the drunken swordplay?

t_ch: …我不会，不是喝了这个烈焰浓茶就能会吗？
t_en: ...No, I figured I could just learn it by drinking Flaming Potent Tea.

t_ch: 三月小姐，幻戏和现实是不一样的。
t_en: That's not how it works in real life, Miss March.

t_ch: 三月，你还有些其他的本事吧？
t_en: Do you have any other tricks up your sleeve, March?

t_ch: 你说的对，我在江湖上可是学了不少！
t_en: Absolutely! I've learned a lot from my adventures!

t_ch: 两位师父，我们还是先考试吧。
t_en: Let's focus on the test, masters.

t_ch: 就算不会「醉剑」，我也学了些其他的本事！
t_en: Even if I don't know drunken swordplay, I've got other tricks up my sleeve!

t_ch: 我听说三月小姐最近游历了不少，至于其中有多少能化为剑招，只能拭目以待了。
t_en: I've heard you've traveled a lot, Miss March. I'm excited to see how you incorporate your experiences into your swordplay moves.

t_ch: 三月七，这次的对手与公司机甲相近，你取出双剑，好好应战吧！
t_en: Your opponent this time is similar to the IPC mechs. Unsheathe your swords and give it everything you've got!

t_ch: 呼——还真是不容易！
t_en: Whew... that was tough!

t_ch: 真是一些奇奇怪怪的本事，不过能如此自然地将它们运用在剑招之中，这足以被称为三月小姐自己的独门绝技了。
t_en: Those moves were pretty unconventional, but the way you blended them into your swordplay was impressive. It's like you've developed your own unique style, Miss March.

t_ch: 本以为三月小姐在云璃的鼓动下四处游玩是浪费时间，看来也不全是浪费。
t_en: So, I thought you were just wasting your time traveling around with Yunli's encouragement, but it seems like it's paid off.

t_ch: 怎么有的师父还在嘴硬？
t_en: Well, well, looks like someone's finally seeing sense.

t_ch: 谢谢二位小师父的指点，那我是顺利通过了考试？是不是？
t_en: Thank you both for your guidance. So, did I pass the test?

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你通过了。
t_en: Without a doubt, you aced it.

t_ch: 好耶！{NICKNAME}，你是不是该夸夸咱了……
t_en: Awesome! Hey, {NICKNAME}, isn't it about time you showered me with praise...

t_ch: 恭喜恭喜，恭喜你！
t_en: Yeah! Congratulations!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这次我就不谦虚了，我真是进步了不少！
t_en: Hehe, I won't be modest this time. My skills have improved by leaps and bounds!

t_ch: 要是下次考试通过，我就表扬。
t_en: I'll save the praise for when you pass the next test.

t_ch: 下次表扬下次的，这次表扬这次的，不许赊账。
t_en: No saving allowed! Let's keep it relevant: This time's for now, next time's for later. No IOUs on praise!

t_ch: 三月好棒！掌握了独门绝技！
t_en: You're amazing, March! You learned your own secret move!

t_ch: 恭喜恭喜，恭喜你！
t_en: Yeah! Congratulations!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这次我就不谦虚了，我真是进步了不少！
t_en: Hehe, I won't be modest this time. My skills have improved by leaps and bounds!

t_ch: 将军之前也同我说过，让我学剑的时候，不能挑剔老师。不止是云骑教习，还包括各种江湖艺人，异邦友人……
t_en: General Jing Yuan once told me that everyone has the potential to be my master, not just Cloud Knight instructors, but also artists and outsiders...

t_ch: 实不相瞒，彦卿就曾被桂乃芬小姐吞过宝剑，那确实是剑术练习中学不到的本事。
t_en: Actually, I was once convinced by Guinaifen into swallowing a sword. That's definitely not something you learn from regular practice.

t_ch: 现在，三月觉得自己有几分胜算了？
t_en: So, what do you think your odds are, March?

t_ch: 三分、四分？
t_en: Maybe around thirty... or forty percent?

t_ch: 这次你好像没有上次那么自信？
t_en: You don't seem as confident as last time. What's up?

t_ch: 这说明我的剑术理解加深了，已经会估算实力了。
t_en: Well, I've gained a deeper understanding of swordplay, and I'm more aware of my own strengths now.

t_ch: 顺带一提，幻戏里大侠在开战之前都会隐藏实力的。
t_en: By the way, in those immersia stories, the swordmasters always hide their true strength before a decisive battle.

t_ch: 毕竟一般说「会赢的」都输了。
t_en: Yep, those who boast "they'd win" usually end up losing.

t_ch: 哎，这就是经验之谈啊。
t_en: Yeah, that's usually how it goes.

t_ch: 那你刚才是不是随便说的胜算。
t_en: So, did you just randomly throw out a number for your odds?

t_ch: 被你发现了。
t_en: Oh, you got me.

t_ch: 那你能赶上约定之日的决斗吗？
t_en: Will you be ready for the final showdown?

t_ch: 事在人为吧。
t_en: Hope for the best, and prepare for the worst.

t_ch: 你真是三月七吗？
t_en: Are you really March 7th?

t_ch: 怎么了嘛，三月七也可以说一些故作高深的话！
t_en: Hey, even I can say things that sound profound and mysterious!

t_ch: 你不会真以为我傻了吧唧的吧！
t_en: You don't really think I'm dumb, do you?

t_ch: 这句话也是幻戏里的？
t_en: Is that another line from an immersia?

t_ch: 没错，你很敏锐嘛。
t_en: Yep, you're sharp.

t_ch: 从刚才的考试能看得出来，你离融汇贯通还需要一些时间。不过能做到现在这样已经很棒了，今天我来请你们喝奶茶！
t_en: From the test, I can tell that you still need some time to master your swordplay, but you did pretty well. Milk tea is on me today!

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yeah!

t_ch: 过了不久，罗浮之上某个阴暗的角落里……
t_en: Moments later, in a dark corner on the Luofu...

t_ch: 无名客学剑的进度汇报完毕。斯科特专员，现在没有什么别的工作要做了吗？
t_en: The progress report on the Nameless girl's sword training is complete. What should I do now, Specialist Skott?

t_ch: 哈——？！你是在讽刺我？
t_en: Huh...!? Are you being sarcastic or something?

t_ch: 我是说，我没有别的工作安排了吗？我一直监视别人小姑娘学剑，感觉有些浪费时间，心里有些过意不去……
t_en: I mean, don't I have other important tasks to do? It feels like a bit of a waste of time to keep tabs on some random girl's swordplay training...

t_ch: 你那是浪费时间吗？你是在保障星际和平公司的信誉，琥珀王的荣耀！
t_en: A waste of time? No, this is about safeguarding the reputation of the IPC and the honor of the Amber Lord!

t_ch: 你是说那个三月七已经能用剑击倒工造司的金人了？啧啧啧，区区仙舟金人，哪里比得上公司的机甲！
t_en: Are you saying that March 7th can actually take down an aurumaton from the Artisanship Commission with a sword? Come on, an aurumaton is no match for our state-of-the-art mechs!

t_ch: 你去把现有的机甲加厚加固，我不相信那小铁片子真的能碰瓷我们的尖端科技！
t_en: Go and strengthen our mechs even further! I refuse to believe that little sliver of metal can actually challenge our cutting-edge technology!

t_ch: 可是，护甲已经很厚了。
t_en: But the armor is already pretty thick.

t_ch: 不够，继续给我加！丢我的脸就相当于丢公司的脸，丢琥珀王的脸，你们丢得起吗？
t_en: No, it's not enough! Reinforce it more! If I lose face, it's like losing face for the IPC and the Amber Lord! Can we afford to let that happen?
Understood...

t_ch: 在考试之前，有一件事我要告诉你，三月小姐，我收到了斯科特的战帖。
t_en: Before we start the test, there's something I need to tell you, Miss March. I've received a challenge letter from Skott.

t_ch: 他做得很周全，早早联系了地衡司，提交了「武术交流活动」的审批…还将比斗地点选在金人巷，看来那里与他也算是颇有渊源吧。
t_en: He's made thorough preparations, contacting the Realm-Keeping Commission and getting approval for a "martial arts exchange event"... He even chose Aurum Alley as the venue. Seems like he has some connections there.

t_ch: 不过按照规矩，收到战帖的人仍然可以拒不接受挑战。
t_en: However, according to the rules, the person receiving the challenge letter has the right to decline the challenge.

t_ch: 那不就是承认自己会输嘛，我肯定接受啊！
t_en: Declining would be like admitting defeat! Of course I'll accept it!

t_ch: 在那之前，三月小姐还有最后一次考试——也就是「出师试」。
t_en: But before that, you need to pass the final test, the graduation exam.

t_ch: 三月，你这么快就出师了？
t_en: You're graduating already, March?

t_ch: 我也很惊讶。
t_en: I'm surprised, too.

t_ch: 彦卿师父，你是不想教了？
t_en: Is it that you don't want to teach her anymore, Master Yanqing?

t_ch: 不会吧，彦卿师父对我可好了。
t_en: No way. Master Yanqing has been great to me.

t_ch: 这倒也不是真正的出师，只不过这一次考试的对手，是你的师父——彦卿。
t_en: Well, it's not really graduation. It's just that the opponent for this exam is your master, Yanqing.

t_ch: 将军教我学习剑术，每到关键的节点，他便会亲自下场与我对练，测试我水平如何。
t_en: When General Jing Yuan was teaching me swordplay, he used to personally spar with me during crucial moments to assess my skills.

t_ch: 按理来说，师父最明白弟子有什么做得好、又有什么不足。
t_en: After all, the master should be the one who is most aware of their student's strengths and weaknesses.

t_ch: 彦卿师父，和你交手…我真的能打赢吗？
t_en: But Master Yanqing, facing you in a duel... do I really stand a chance?

t_ch: 不试试怎么知道呢？难道说，对手是我，三月小姐就怕了吗？
t_en: How will we ever find out if you don't even try? Are you only scared because I'm your opponent, Miss March?

t_ch: 三月小姐，取出双剑，摆好架势，要开始了！
t_en: Unsheathe your swords and get ready, Miss March! The duel starts now!

t_ch: 不错，三月小姐撑到了现在，你通过了。
t_en: Not bad! You've made it this far, Miss March. You've passed the exam.

t_ch: 哦哦哦哦哦！
t_en: Ohhhhhhh!

t_ch: 恭喜恭喜，恭喜你！
t_en: Yeah! Congratulations!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我说过，我是越夸越会优秀的类型嘛。
t_en: Hehe, I told you compliments make me better.

t_ch: 考试结束，真正的考验才开始。
t_en: The tests are all over, but the real trial is just about to begin.

t_ch: 虽然你说的挺有道理的，但这可不是糊弄我的时候，快夸夸我。
t_en: I get your point, but this isn't the time for deflections — quickly, say something nice about me!

t_ch: 恭喜三月加入战胜彦卿俱乐部。
t_en: Congrats! You're now officially a member of the "Bested Yanqing" club!

t_ch: 实际上，我并没有战胜彦卿师父……
t_en: Actually, I didn't defeat Master Yanqing...

t_ch: 恭喜恭喜，恭喜你！
t_en: Yeah! Congratulations!

t_ch: 这句话听多了，我都会唱了。
t_en: Oh, I've heard that line so many times before!

t_ch: 三月，凭你眼下的剑术，即便面对公司的机甲，也能稳操胜券。
t_en: With your swordplay skills, you can effortlessly take down IPC mechs, March.

t_ch: 我也有这样的信心。
t_en: Yeah, I'm pretty confident too.

t_ch: 但你现在再回头看看，是不是有了比「剑术」更重要的收获？
t_en: But looking back, haven't you learned something more important than just swordplay?

t_ch: 这段时间，咱不仅向两位老师学了剑，还认识了很多人和事，得到很多宝贵的体验。
t_en: Yeah, I've not only learned swordplay from masters, but I've also met a bunch of people and gained a lot of insights from my experiences.

t_ch: 这些都将成为你剑术的「养分」。就像是你的云璃师父说过的，「这大千世界，无处不是剑，无处不是剑招」。
t_en: Those experiences will nourish your sword skills. It's just like Master Yunli said, "Everything can be a sword, and there are endless possibilities for learning swordplay in the vast world."

t_ch: …好像确实是这样。
t_en: ...That actually makes sense.

t_ch: 现在，关于如何击败斯科特，我想听听你的看法。
t_en: Now, I'd love to hear your suggestions on how to defeat Skott.

t_ch: 堂堂正正，用剑术一决胜负。
t_en: Defeat him fair and square with your sword.

t_ch: 嗯，没错，就让斯科特见识见识他瞧不起的仙舟剑术！
t_en: Yeah! I'll show Skott the power of the Xianzhou swordplay that he despises!

t_ch: 也许，我们还有别的致胜法…
t_en: Maybe there's another method we can use...

t_ch: 我明白了，就这样做吧！想要击败他，何必非要剑术呢？
t_en: I see, let's give it a shot! Who says we have to rely purely on swordplay to beat him?

t_ch: （去找藿藿）
t_en: (Ask Huohuo for help)

t_ch: （去找椒丘）
t_en: (Ask Jiaoqiu for help)

t_ch: （去找银狼）
t_en: (Ask Silver Wolf for help)

t_ch: （去找青雀）
t_en: (Ask Qingque for help)

t_ch: 嘿嘿，就这样做，我已经有想法了。
t_en: Hehe, let's do it. I already have an idea.

t_ch: 我记得当时彦卿说过「战胜一群恶徒的方法有很多，不过你若想成为『英雄大侠』，除了提升自己没有任何捷径可以走。」
t_en: I remember Yanqing once said, "There are many ways to defeat villains, but if you want to become a true hero, you have to focus on self-improvement."

t_ch: 三月七，现在你觉得自己离你理想的「英雄大侠」更接近了吗？
t_en: Do you feel any closer to becoming the great hero you've always dreamed of?

t_ch: 当然！我心里的「英雄大侠」会这样做的！
t_en: Yes, absolutely! The great hero within me is definitely up to the task!

t_ch: 瞧瞧，这不是我们的女侠三月七吗？
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't our hero, March 7th!

t_ch: 哼，本姑娘没有躲没有藏，你下了战帖，我自会前来应战。
t_en: Hmph, I'm not hiding or backing down. You challenged me, and I'm here to accept it.

t_ch: …不过咱就是问一下，你干嘛非得选在金人巷里？这里游人还挺多的，要是有人被误伤到了也不好。
t_en: ...But I'm curious, why pick Aurum Alley of all places? It's packed with tourists around, and it'd be bad if someone got hurt.

t_ch: 为什么是金人巷？你身边这位{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}最清楚不过了。
t_en: Why Aurum Alley? Well, your buddy knows the answer to that.

t_ch: 毕竟，这是你学狗叫的地方。
t_en: Well, this is the place you lost a bet and barked like a dog, after all.

t_ch: 我无法否认，我始终忘不掉那一天的事情。
t_en: I can't deny it. That fateful day left a mark on me.

t_ch: 你在这里遭遇了事业的挫折。
t_en: Because you took a hit to your career that day.

t_ch: 没错，从那天起，我被抽调出分部的核心业务。
t_en: Exactly. On that fateful day, I was reassigned, dismissed from my department's core operations.

t_ch: 我特意选在金人巷，那是因为我得让当初那些嘲笑我的父老乡亲们前来围观。
t_en: That's why I chose this place. I want to show all those people who laughed at me that I can stand on my own two feet again.

t_ch: 他们会惊讶「啊——？！当初那个斯科特怎么又回来了？这次他又要做什么？」
t_en: They'll be like, "What...!? That Skott is back again? What's he got up to this time?"

t_ch: 他们会看到，当初被当众羞辱的斯科特重新回到了金人巷一雪前耻。
t_en: They'll see that I, Skott, who was once shamed in front of everyone, is back in Aurum Alley to restore his honor!

t_ch: 我得让父老乡亲们听个明白，今天在这里学小畜生的，不会是我，而是你——！
t_en: They'll see that it won't be me squealing like a pig today, but YOU!

t_ch: 感觉你的格局太小了。
t_en: Seems kinda petty, don't you think?

t_ch: 是啊，要是幻戏里的反派就这点追求，我肯定是要退票的。
t_en: Yeah, if that's all a villain did in an immersia, I'd definitely be asking for a refund.

t_ch: 是不是找错复仇对象了？
t_en: Are you even seeking revenge on the right person?

t_ch: 错，我要复仇的是整个金人巷！
t_en: Right person? No, I want to enact revenge on the entirety of Aurum Alley!

t_ch: …可是你复仇金人巷，与欺负不夜侯的小老板娘有什么关系？
t_en: ...But then wait, where does bullying the owner of the Sleepless Earl fit in with all of this?

t_ch: 闭嘴——！
t_en: Ugh! Shut up!

t_ch: 我有自己的原则，我不想一辈子都陷在金人巷的阴影里！你以为我是公司臭要饭的？
t_en: Look, I have my principles. I refuse to spend my life haunted by what happened in Aurum Alley! Do you think I'm just some sort of IPC small fry?

t_ch: 我忍了这么久，就是想等一个机会！我要争一口气，不是要证明我有多了不起，我是要告诉金人巷里的所有人，我失去的东西，我一定要拿回来！
t_en: I've been patient for so long, just waiting for the right moment! I don't care about proving my greatness — it's about showing everyone in Aurum Alley that I will reclaim what was taken from me!

t_ch: 少废话，你失去的拿不回去了！
t_en: Spare me the lecture! You're not getting back what you lost!

t_ch: 就是就是！
t_en: No, you won't!

t_ch: 你应该堂堂正正拿回你的尊严！
t_en: You should reclaim your dignity in a more respectable way!

t_ch: 而不是用这种肮脏的手段！
t_en: Instead of resorting to such despicable methods!

t_ch: 我调查过了，你不过是学剑才十五天的菜鸟，就这还想打败我无敌的机甲？痴人说梦！
t_en: I've been keeping an eye on you. Seriously, do you really believe that you, a rookie who's only been learning swordplay for fifteen days, can defeat my invincible mechs? Dream on!

t_ch: 客套话就到此为止了！兄弟们，给我上！
t_en: Enough with the talk! Let's get down to business, boys!

t_ch: 哼，等你输了以后，你就知道本姑娘到底有什么本事了！
t_en: Hah! Once you bite the dust, you'll know what I'm capable of!

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊——你们这些好吃懒做的废物！你们怎么、怎么就输给了那个菜鸟了呢？！
t_en: Ahhhhhhhh! You pathetic losers! How... how did you all lose to that rookie!?
